reasoning:
1. Base Rate & Trend: The most recent known value (as of 2025-06-20) is 4.89%. The FRED statistical projection indicates a 46% probability of exceeding this value by late June 2025.
2. Fundamentals: Recent reports (June 2025) note that expected Federal Reserve rate cuts are currently keeping views on Treasury yields low, countering the upward pressure from budget deficit fears that previously pushed the 30-year yield up to ~5.09% in May. A Reuters consensus poll anticipates the 10-year yield will fall over the next 3 to 6 months. This anticipated easing points to an expected downward trajectory for long-term yields.
3. Term Structure of Uncertainty: While the expected value of the yield is forecast to be lower than current levels, variance technically expands over longer horizons. As strictly instructed to ensure uncertainty increases with horizon (i.e., probabilities for distant dates move closer to 0.5), I start with a short-term probability moderately below 0.5 (reflecting the downward drift) and regress it step-by-step toward 0.5 for the longest date.
